title: dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. lobelii
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. lobelii, commonly known as the marsh orchid, is a subspecies of terrestrial orchid that is native to the wetlands of Europe and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The marsh orchid is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the roots of Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. lobelii are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
